summary: The Interview Center page (/dashboard/interviews) never calls any of its 7 documented backend endpoints. It is a fully static placeholder — even after real InterviewSchedule rows exist in the database for the logged-in user, the page renders the exact same hardcoded "No interview scheduled" empty state.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 at https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/login.

2. Via API, POST /api/interviews with a valid application_id (e.g. {"application_id":"cc3aaae4343a969731915097b","type":"video","scheduled_at":"2026-08-01T10:00:00Z","notes":"MV-interview-center-001 create test"}) — returns 201 with a real interview row.
3. Navigate the browser to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/interviews with devtools Network tab open.

4. Observe the only /api/* calls fired are shared dashboard-shell calls (agents, approvals, billing/entitlement, workspaces/settings) — GET /interviews and GET /workspaces/interviews/prep are never requested.
5. Observe the page still shows 'No interview scheduled. Once an application progresses to interview stage, your prep brief appears here.' despite the interview existing.

6. Read apps/web/src/app/dashboard/interviews/page.tsx (repo, same commit) — it is a static component with no useEffect, no fetch, no API client import. A prior richer version (git commit 6851cdf) that called fetchInterviewPrep() from apps/web/src/lib/api/workspaces.ts was deliberately reverted to this static placeholder in commit 0701b34 ('replace crashing Interview Center with stable placeholder').
expected: The screen should call GET /workspaces/interviews/prep (and ideally GET /interviews) on load and render real session/brief/questions/liveAssist/debrief data when present, per the wireframe and per the still-live, working backend endpoint.
observed: Page is 100% static HTML/JSX with no data fetching. Confirmed twice (fresh sessions) via network capture: 0 requests to /api/interviews or /api/workspaces/interviews/prep on load, even with live InterviewSchedule data present for the account.

summary: Production Interview Center is visually a bare empty-state card (icon + 2 lines of text + one button). Almost every component in the wireframe (design/screens/interview-center.html) is absent: the Prep/Live Assist/Debrief tab bar, the compliance & consent banner, Company & Role Brief panel, Predicted Questions list, Live Assist panel with mute toggle and filler-word/pace/talk-listen metrics, and the Last Debrief snapshot card with score and 'View full debrief' button.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/dashboard/interviews.
3. Compare against design/screens/interview-center.html (data-design-ids: tabs-ic05, compliance-banner-ic17, prep-main-ic09, pq-card-ic10/11/12, live-assist-ic14, btn-mute-ic18, debrief-snap-ic15).
4. None of those elements exist in the DOM at all (confirmed via page.content() dump).
expected: Wireframe shows a rich 3-column interview-prep workspace with tabs, live coaching metrics, and a debrief history snapshot for an in-progress interview, plus (by extension) an honest empty state matching that structure when no interview exists.
observed: Only the page title, a one-line subtitle, and a generic centered empty-state card with a single 'View Applications' CTA are rendered — no tabs, no compliance banner, no brief, no live assist, no debrief exist in any state reachable from the UI (confirmed empty-state is the ONLY state reachable, per MV-interview-center-001).

summary: There is no control anywhere in the production app — not on the Interview Center screen, not on the Applications screen — to schedule/create an interview. The full backend CRUD (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /interviews, /interviews/{id}/complete, /interviews/{id}/cancel) is implemented, correct, and reachable only via direct API calls; a paying user has no way to reach any of it through the product UI. The Applications screen's interview-stage card link ('View in CRM') routes to /dashboard/networking, not to Interview Center, and no 'Schedule interview' affordance exists anywhere.
reproduction: 2. Visit /dashboard/interviews — only element is 'View Applications' link; no create/schedule button.

3. Visit /dashboard/applications — inspect an interview-stage application card; its only cross-link ('View in CRM') goes to /dashboard/networking (apps/web/src/app/dashboard/applications/page.tsx, CardLink() function), not to Interview Center or any schedule-interview form.
4. Grep the whole apps/web/src tree for any caller of the interviews API — none found besides the unused fetchInterviewPrep() helper in lib/api/workspaces.ts.
expected: A paying user should be able to schedule an interview (from an application card or from Interview Center directly) and see it reflected in the Interview Center prep view, per the wireframe's premise of an active, in-progress interview.
observed: No UI entry point exists to create InterviewSchedule rows. The feature is fully backend-complete but 100% unreachable from the product, i.e. dead on arrival for end users despite working infrastructure.

summary: POST /interviews does not validate that application_id references an existing Application row owned by the caller. Supplying a nonexistent application_id succeeds with HTTP 201 and creates a permanently orphaned InterviewSchedule row (InterviewSchedule.applicationId has no FK constraint — apps/api/app/routers/interviews.py CREATE TABLE, column is plain `text NOT NULL`).
reproduction: 1. Authenticate as admin/admin123, obtain bearer token.
2. POST https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/api/interviews with body {"application_id":"MV-interview-center-verify2-bogus-app","scheduled_at":"2026-08-11T03:00:00Z"} (no such application exists).
3. Observe HTTP 201 Created with a full interview object referencing the bogus application_id.
4. GET /interviews confirms the orphaned row is listed like any real one.
Reproduced twice in independent sessions (interview ids c4fcb3bb3f5c4c245c37117a3 and ca138a62b952fae2b79811283), both times 201; both cleaned up via DELETE afterward.
expected: Creating an interview against a non-existent application should fail with 404/422, matching the pattern used elsewhere in the codebase (e.g. resume/job FK checks) — the code comment in InterviewCreate even states application_id 'is NOT NULL' is enforced 'so a missing value must 422... never reach the INSERT and crash', implying referential validity was intended but only presence, not existence, is checked.
observed: Any string is accepted as application_id; the row inserts successfully, is retrievable/editable/completable/cancelable/deletable like a normal interview, silently orphaned from any real application (this endpoint is not reachable via the current UI, so real-world exposure is limited to internal tooling / a future UI regaining access to POST /interviews).
